articles.title: Delhi Rent Control Act, A Case for Reference To A Larger Bench
articles.summary: The Delhi Rent Control Act, 1958 (DRC Act) was enacted as a situational legislation in the aftermath of the Partition of India which led to a temporary housing shortage. Being a socio-economic legislation, it required periodic review to remain in consonance with the changing times. However, the Executive's inaction in not issuing a commencement notification to enforce the Delhi Rent Act, 1995, coupled with judicial deference, has led to DRC Act assuming permanence. articles.title: Vijay oath-taking confusion: Governor’s role, floor test rules and past Supreme Court judgment
articles.summary: The article examines the role of the Governor in appointing a Chief Minister when a single party has an absolute majority in the Assembly, citing the Sarkaria Commission Report (1983). It highlights the constitutional and procedural rules governing such situations, including the requirement for a floor test. The discussion is relevant to India's governance framework and may influence future political appointments and legislative procedures.
articles.raw_summary: As per the Sarkaria Commission Report (1983), which discusses the role of the governor, if a single party has an absolute majority in the Assembly, the leader of that party should automatically be asked to become the Chief Minister.

The proposal is under consideration at the central government level, with potential implications for India's trade and foreign policy strategies.

articles.title: Benami Veil Cannot Be Lifted by Clever Drafting: SC Restores Rejection of Plaint at Threshold
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that courts must thoroughly examine plaintiffs' claims and reject lawsuits that indirectly seek to enforce benami (prohibited) transactions, even if drafted cleverly. The decision strengthens legal safeguards under Order VII Rule 11 of the Civil Procedure Code to prevent sham litigation. This ruling impacts financial policy enforcement and litigation involving benami transactions in India.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court held that courts must undertake a meaningful reading of the plaint and reject suits that indirectly seek enforcement of prohibited benami transactions. The ruling strengthens Order VII Rule 11 CPC as a safeguard against sham and legally barred litigation. The post Benami Veil Cannot Be Lifted by Clever Drafting: SC Restores Rejection of Plaint at Threshold appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Supreme Court Orders Criminal Action Against Constable Who Joined Bihar & Jharkhand Police In Different Names
articles.summary: The Supreme Court directed criminal action against a constable who joined the Bihar and Jharkhand police forces using different names. The Court stated that such conduct severely undermines the credibility of the police force. The ruling highlights systemic issues in personnel verification and integrity within state police departments.
articles.raw_summary: The continuance of such an employee is "wholly detrimental to the credibility of the police force", the Court held.

articles.title: Supreme Court Directs Centre To Form 1 Exclusive NIA Court For Every 10-15 Cases, Urges To Complete At Least 1 Trial Per Month
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has directed the central government to establish one exclusive National Investigation Agency (NIA) court for every 10-15 cases. These courts must conduct trials on a day-to-day basis and should not be assigned other cases. The Court has urged that at least one trial be completed per month in these courts.
articles.raw_summary: The Court directed that no other cases must be entrusted to the exclusive NIA Courts, which are to conduct trials on a day-to-day basis.

articles.title: Family and Personal Law April 2026: SC Quashes 80+ Litigations; HC Invokes “Ardhangini” to Appoint Wife as Guardian, Bars RTI for Spousal Tax Details and More
articles.summary: The Supreme Court quashed over 80 litigations related to family and personal law in April 2026. The High Court invoked the concept of 'Ardhangini' (meaning 'better half') to appoint a wife as her husband's guardian. Additionally, the court barred the Right to Information (RTI) from accessing spousal tax details. The article also highlights other key judgments on marriage, divorce, maintenance, child custody, and waqf.

articles.title: PF & ESI Disallowance Removed as Income Tax Act 2025 allows Deposit till ITR Due Date
articles.summary: The Income Tax Act 2025 now allows employers to deposit employee contributions to Provident Fund (PF) and Employees' State Insurance (ESI) until the due date for filing Income Tax Returns (ITR). Previously, even a one-day delay in depositing these contributions resulted in permanent tax disallowance. This change provides relief to businesses by removing the disallowance if payments are made before the ITR due date.
articles.raw_summary: Under the earlier law, even a one-day delay in depositing employee contributions resulted in permanent tax disallowance. articles.title: Why Madras HC ordered granting full maternity leave to judicial employee for third pregnancy
articles.summary: The Madras High Court ruled that a judicial employee should receive full maternity leave for her third pregnancy, criticizing a 12-week limit as discriminatory. The court stated that suffering during pregnancy is the same regardless of the number of pregnancies and that a welfare state cannot deny equal benefits. The ruling highlights the need for equitable maternity leave policies in government employment.
articles.raw_summary: The court criticised a 12-week maternity leave limit on third pregnancies, saying 'suffering would be the same' and a 'welfare state' cannot discriminate in approving maternity benefits.

articles.title: IBC Is Not a Recovery Shortcut: SC Rejects CIRP in Builder-Bank-Borrower Dispute
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that insolvency proceedings under the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (IBC) cannot be used as a shortcut to recover disputed dues from contractual transactions. The case involved a dispute between a builder, a bank, and a borrower. The court emphasized that the IBC is not a recovery mechanism but a process for resolving insolvency. This ruling clarifies the scope of the IBC and its limitations in commercial disputes.

articles.title: Filing of Form 10-IE for New Tax Regime Is Directory, Not Mandatory: ITAT Ahmedabad
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) Ahmedabad ruled that filing Form 10-IE for the new tax regime is directory (not mandatory) and cannot be ignored solely due to a missed deadline. The tribunal directed the Centralized Processing Centre (CPC) to recompute tax under the new regime. The decision impacts taxpayers opting for the new tax regime and clarifies procedural compliance requirements.
articles.raw_summary: The ITAT Ahmedabad held that Form 10-IE filed along with the return could not be ignored merely because it was not filed within the due date under Section 139(1). The Tribunal directed CPC to recompute tax under the new tax regime. The post Filing of Form 10-IE for New Tax Regime Is Directory, Not Mandatory: ITAT Ahmedabad appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Is The Current Tax System Really Fair or it Becoming Too Harsh?

It highlights concerns like high compliance costs, cash flow issues, and layered taxation, suggesting these factors may be placing excessive pressure on individuals and businesses. The discussion is framed as a critique of current tax policies rather than a report on new government actions.

articles.title: CIT(A) Is Not a Dismissal Counter: ITAT Says Appeals Must Be Decided on Merits, Not Closed for Non-Compliance
articles.summary: The Pune Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that appellate authorities like the Commissioner of Income Tax (Appeals) [CIT(A)] cannot dismiss appeals purely due to procedural non-compliance. Instead, they must adjudicate the merits of the case. The tribunal restored a dismissed appeal for fresh consideration, emphasizing that appeals should not be treated as 'dismissal counters.' This decision impacts how tax appeals are handled and reinforces the need for substantive review over mechanic
articles.raw_summary: Pune ITAT held that appellate authorities must adjudicate issues raised in appeals and cannot mechanically dismiss matters for non-compliance. The Tribunal restored the case for fresh consideration on merits. The post CIT(A) Is Not a Dismissal Counter: ITAT Says Appeals Must Be Decided on Merits, Not Closed for Non-Compliance appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: CESTAT Allows Excise Appeal as Trade Discount Not Proven as Additional Consideration
articles.summary: The Customs, Excise and Service Tax Appellate Tribunal (CESTAT) ruled that a trade discount given to a bulk buyer cannot be added to the assessable value for excise duty without proof of additional consideration. The tribunal found that the tax department failed to demonstrate any free benefit flowing from the buyer to the seller. This decision impacts how excise duty is calculated on commercial transactions involving bulk discounts.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that a commercial trade discount given to a bulk buyer could not be added to assessable value without evidence of additional consideration. It ruled that the department failed to prove any free benefit flowing from the buyer to the assessee. The post CESTAT Allows Excise Appeal as Trade Discount Not Proven as Additional Consideration appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Section 14A Disallowance Deleted as AO Failed to Record Objective Satisfaction: ITAT Agra
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) in Agra ruled that a Section 14A disallowance was invalid because the Assessing Officer (AO) failed to record objective satisfaction before applying Rule 8D. The tribunal emphasized that such satisfaction is mandatory under tax law. This decision impacts how tax disallowances are applied in cases involving exempt income.
articles.raw_summary: The ITAT Agra upheld deletion of a Section 14A disallowance after finding that the Assessing Officer mechanically applied Rule 8D without recording reasons for dissatisfaction. The Tribunal reiterated that such satisfaction is mandatory before invoking Rule 8D. The post Section 14A Disallowance Deleted as AO Failed to Record Objective Satisfaction: ITAT Agra appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Labour reforms: Govt fully operationalises four new codes by publishing rules
articles.summary: India's central government has fully implemented four new labour codes. These codes, effective November 21, 2025, consolidate 29 laws. They aim to provide minimum wage and universal social security for all workers. The new framework enhances worker protection and eases business operations. Key provisions include mandatory appointment letters and a National Reskilling Fund.
articles.raw_summary: India's central government has fully implemented four new labour codes. These codes, effective November 21, 2025, consolidate 29 laws. They aim to provide minimum wage and universal social security for all workers. The new framework enhances worker protection and eases business operations. Key provisions include mandatory appointment letters and a National Reskilling Fund.

articles.title: Place of supply under GST regarding Immovable Property Services with FAQs
articles.summary: The article explains that services related to immovable property, such as architectural and accommodation services, fall under Section 12(3) of the Integrated Goods and Services Tax (IGST) Act. It clarifies that the place of supply for these services is determined by the location of the property, not the recipient. This rule impacts how GST is applied to such transactions. The article is published by TaxGuru.
articles.raw_summary: The article explains that architectural, accommodation, and related immovable property services are governed by Section 12(3) of the IGST Act. It clarifies that the place of supply is linked to the property location rather than the recipient’s location. The post Place of supply under GST regarding Immovable Property Services with FAQs appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: DPIIT issues revised SOP to make FDI approvals paperless, time-bound
articles.summary: The Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT) has issued a revised Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) to streamline Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) approvals in India. The new rules mandate fully digital filing of applications, set a 12-week deadline for decisions, and introduce tighter scrutiny for investments from land-border countries. articles.title: ITAT Indore Remands TDS Credit Dispute for Verification of Form 16A and Form 26AS
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) in Indore set aside a previous order denying tax deducted at source (TDS) credit to an assessee. The tribunal directed the Assessing Officer to verify the assessee's claims using Form 16A and Form 26AS, which show tax deductions and payments by the deductor. The matter has been remanded for further verification and grant of eligible credit.
articles.raw_summary: ITAT Indore set aside the appellate order denying TDS credit after the assessee produced Form 16A and Form 26AS showing deduction and payment of tax by the deductor. The matter was remanded to the Assessing Officer for verification and grant of eligible credit. The post ITAT Indore Remands TDS Credit Dispute for Verification of Form 16A and Form 26AS appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Section 271(1)(c) Penalty Quashed as Wrong Claim Alone Is Not Concealment: ITAT Rajkot
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) in Rajkot quashed a penalty imposed under Section 271(1)(c) of the Income Tax Act, ruling that a mere disallowance of expenditure during assessment does not automatically constitute concealment or furnishing of inaccurate particulars. The assessee had claimed a wrong deduction, but the tribunal found no intent to evade tax. This decision sets a precedent for future tax penalty cases in India.
articles.raw_summary: The ITAT Rajkot held that mere disallowance of expenditure during assessment proceedings does not automatically justify penalty under Section 271(1)(c). The Tribunal found no concealment or furnishing of inaccurate particulars by the assessee. The post Section 271(1)(c) Penalty Quashed as Wrong Claim Alone Is Not Concealment: ITAT Rajkot appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: RBI officers seek governor's intervention to correct promotion policy
articles.summary: RBI officers are protesting recent changes to the promotion policy. The Reserve Bank of India Officers' Association has written to the Governor seeking intervention. The new policy links promotions to vacancy availability, replacing the earlier time-bound system. This has caused widespread dissatisfaction and demoralization among officers. The association demands an immediate review and the reintroduction of assured, time-bound promotions.

articles.title: Right To Property Under Article 300-A Cannot Be Defeated By Erroneous Revenue Entries: Bombay High Court
articles.summary: The Bombay High Court has held that the right to property under Article 300-A of the Constitution cannot be defeated on the basis of erroneous revenue entries made during the implementation of a consolidation scheme. The Court observed that where landholders were not issued notice as required under Section 15A of the Maharashtra Prevention of Fragmentation and Consolidation of Holdings Act, 1947, delay in seeking correction of revenue entries cannot be used to deny restoration of their holdings.

articles.summary: The Kerala High Court has held that in prosecutions under the Protection of Children from Sexual Offences Act, 2012 (POCSO Act), conviction may be sustained even in the absence of documentary proof of age when the oral testimony of the child victim and her mother regarding the age of the Victim remain unchallenged during trial. articles.title: States told to shift to new base year for GSDP by end of FY27
articles.summary: The statistics ministry has instructed all states to adopt 2022-23 as the new base year for calculating Gross State Domestic Product (GSDP) by the end of fiscal year 2027. This change aims to align state-level economic data with the revised national GDP framework, improving accuracy. States are also permitted to develop sub-state Consumer Price Index (CPI) measures, though no central directive mandates this.
articles.raw_summary: The statistics ministry has directed states to adopt 2022-23 as the new base year for Gross State Domestic Product (GSDP) computation by fiscal 2027. This move aligns state-level estimates with the revised national GDP framework, enhancing regional economic data accuracy. While states are also free to develop sub-state CPI, there's no central directive for this.

articles.title: India should adopt dual-track trade strategy at WTO, say experts
articles.summary: Experts suggest India adopt a dual-track trade strategy. This involves defending World Trade Organisation rules while selectively joining plurilateral talks. Such initiatives shape global trade. India has concerns about plurilateral agreements weakening the multilateral system. However, recalibrating its approach could serve India's economic and strategic interests. Pragmatic, coalition-based approaches are needed to revitalize multilateralism.

articles.title: No Addition, No Under-Reporting: ITAT Deletes Section 270A Penalty When Income Returned u/s 148 Was Fully Accepted
articles.summary: The Pune Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that no penalty for under-reporting income can be imposed if the Assessing Officer accepts the income declared in response to a Section 148 notice without any additions. The Tribunal deleted the entire penalty under Section 270A in this case. This decision clarifies the legal interpretation of tax penalties and reinforces the principle that penalties require actual under-reporting, not just a reassessment.
articles.raw_summary: Pune ITAT held that penalty for under-reporting of income cannot survive where the Assessing Officer accepts the income declared in response to notice under Section 148 without any addition. The Tribunal deleted the entire penalty under Section 270A. The post No Addition, No Under-Reporting: ITAT Deletes Section 270A Penalty When Income Returned u/s 148 Was Fully Accepted appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: E-Challan Issued Through Personal Mobile Phone Of Police Officer Is Procedurally Defective: Srinagar Court
articles.summary: A Srinagar trial court quashed multiple e-challans issued against a vehicle owner, ruling they were procedurally defective. The court held that the challans were not generated through officially authenticated devices, violating Rule 167A of the Central Motor Vehicles Rules, 1989. Judge Shabir Ahmad Malik observed that issuing challans via personal mobile phones lacked mandatory safeguards. The ruling sets a precedent for proper enforcement procedures in traffic violations.

articles.context_summary: India’s retail inflation likely rose to 3.8% in April from 3.4% in March as higher fuel and LPG costs linked to the US-Iran conflict started feeding into prices, according to a Reuters poll. Economists said food prices remained soft, helping keep inflation near the RBI’s 4% target, but persistently high crude oil prices and a possible weak monsoon could push inflation higher in coming months. Wholesale inflation is also expected to rise to 4.4% in April.

articles.title: ITAT Deletes Section 50C Addition as Stamp Value Difference Was Within 5% Safe Harbour
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) in Indore ruled that Section 50C of the Income Tax Act cannot be applied when the difference between the actual sale price of a property and its stamp duty valuation is within 5%. In this case, the difference was only 1.19%, so the tribunal directed the use of the actual sale consideration for calculating capital gains. This decision clarifies the application of tax rules for property transactions and sets a precedent for similar cases.
articles.raw_summary: The ITAT Indore held that Section 50C could not be invoked where the difference between actual sale consideration and stamp duty valuation was only 1.19%. The Tribunal directed adoption of actual sale consideration as the full value for capital gains computation. The post ITAT Deletes Section 50C Addition as Stamp Value Difference Was Within 5% Safe Harbour appeared first on TaxGuru .

The police action occurred without formal charges or due process.
articles.raw_summary: Workers who were detained and released afterwards face no legal charges but are stranded without access to the information on their mobiles.

The banks argue that inflation and higher borrowing costs must be considered in these definitions.

articles.title: Freebie burden may strain state finances, push borrowing higher: Economists
articles.summary: Economists warn that rising welfare spending by Indian states, including subsidies and freebies, could strain state finances and force cuts to capital expenditure. M R Madhavan, Co-founder of PRS Legislative Research, highlights that a large portion of state revenues is already committed to salaries, pensions, interest payments, and subsidies, leaving little room for discretionary spending. He notes this could ultimately push states to borrow more.
articles.raw_summary: M R Madhavan, Co-founder & Pres, PRS Legislative Research, and who has previously worked as an economist with I-Sec, ICICI and Bank of America, said states may ultimately be forced to squeeze capital expenditure if welfare spending rises while revenues disappoint. He noted that a large portion of state revenues is already committed towards salaries, pensions, interest payments and subsidies, leaving limited room for discretionary spending.

articles.title: India Cannot Sell Its Cotton to the World Until It Admits Why the World Stopped Buying
articles.summary: India's cotton sector has a productivity problem, a quality problem and a market-credibility problem. On Tuesday, the Union Cabinet approved a Rs 5,659.22 crore mission that proposes to address all three simultaneously, through seeds, ginning modernisation, traceability infrastructure and a national branding programme. What the mission does not address may be more revealing than what it does.

What the mission does not address may be more revealing than what it does.

articles.title: Recycled Activewear Reveals the Structural Limits of Fashion’s Sustainability Claims
articles.summary: The article examines the sustainability claims of recycled polyester in activewear, noting that most material still comes from PET drink bottles rather than discarded textiles. This raises concerns about whether fashion brands are truly addressing waste or merely shifting it to other recycling systems. The issue has implications for global textile waste management and could influence future environmental and trade policies in India.
articles.raw_summary: Recycled polyester has become a default sustainability claim across activewear, swimwear and outerwear, but its supply chain is less circular than its labelling suggests. Most of the material still comes from PET drink bottles, not discarded textiles, raising difficult questions about whether fashion is solving waste or diverting it from better-functioning recycling systems globally instead.

articles.title: GSTAT Rejects Mechanical Price Comparison as DGAP Compared Average & Actual Prices Arbitrarily
articles.summary: The Goods and Services Tax Appellate Tribunal (GSTAT) rejected the Directorate General of Anti-Profiteering (DGAP)'s arbitrary comparison of average and actual prices to determine if a company had increased base prices after a GST rate reduction. GSTAT found inconsistencies in DGAP's findings and ordered a comprehensive reinvestigation. The tribunal held that such contradictions required deeper scrutiny. This ruling impacts how anti-profiteering cases under GST are handled.
articles.raw_summary: GSTAT noted inconsistencies in the DGAP’s findings regarding whether the Respondent had actually increased base prices after GST rate reduction. The Tribunal held that such contradictions required comprehensive reinvestigation. The post GSTAT Rejects Mechanical Price Comparison as DGAP Compared Average   Actual Prices Arbitrarily appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Aggregators to be charged interest at 12% per annum if they fail to contribute toward social security of their gig workers
articles.summary: The government has mandated that aggregators (platforms like food delivery or ride-hailing services) must contribute to the social security of gig workers or face a 12% annual interest penalty on delayed payments. All aggregators must upload gig worker details to a central portal within 45 days, with real-time updates for new appointments or exits. These rules aim to ensure gig workers receive timely social security benefits.
articles.raw_summary: Aggregators now face a 12% annual interest penalty for delayed social security contributions for gig workers. All aggregators must upload gig worker details to a central portal within 45 days. New appointments and exits require real-time or daily registration. These rules ensure gig workers receive social security benefits.

articles.title: Kingpin in Kochi forged organ donation documents racket arrested in UP
articles.summary: A key figure involved in a forged organ donation documents racket in Kochi, Kerala, has been arrested in Uttar Pradesh. articles.title: Bakery Products Sold Without Preparation Are Supply of Goods Under GST: Gujarat AAR
articles.summary: The Gujarat Appellate Authority for Advance Ruling (AAR) ruled that bakery items like pizzas, pasta, salads, and shakes prepared or blended at outlets upon customer order are classified as restaurant services under GST, even when sold via takeaway counters. This decision clarifies the tax treatment of such products, distinguishing them from pre-packaged goods. The ruling applies to items prepared on-demand, impacting how bakery outlets are taxed for these services.
articles.raw_summary: The Gujarat AAR ruled that pizzas, pasta, salads, shakes, and similar items prepared or blended at bakery outlets upon customer order constitute restaurant services under GST. This reflects increased expenditure on social welfare programs, particularly food and agricultural subsidies, which may prompt policy reviews or adjustments.

articles.title: Could hantavirus be the new emerging pathogen in Kerala?
articles.summary: Antibodies against hantavirus were detected in Kerala as early as 1999, with a probable case of hantavirus-induced illness reported in 2014. Experts suggest that some 'leptospirosis-like' illnesses with rapid fatal outcomes could be due to hantavirus or co-infections. Symptoms overlap with leptospirosis, making accurate diagnosis difficult without specific tests.
articles.raw_summary: Presence of antibodies against the virus in blood samples was reported in the State in 1999 itself and one probable case of the virus-induced ‘hemorrhagic fever with renal syndrome’ was reported in 2014. Experts say a good number of ‘leptospirosis-like’ illnesses, wherein patients die within four to five days could be due to hantavirus or a co-infection of leptospirosis and hanta. Symptoms are similar and hard to distinguish without targeted serology tests

articles.title: Thali costs rise in April on higher tomato, vegetable oil, LPG prices
articles.summary: The cost of home-cooked vegetarian and non-vegetarian thalis in India rose by 2% year-on-year in April, driven by higher prices of tomatoes, vegetable oil, and LPG. The article highlights inflationary pressures on essential food items, which may prompt government intervention.

Workers and businesses are bearing the brunt of these supply chain disruptions.

The closure of key routes like the Great Lakes Trek has crippled livelihoods, and reopening them is seen as critical for the industry's survival.

articles.title: Census effect: Cities will see ‘less crime’ next year
articles.summary: Crime rates in Indian cities are set to show a decline in 2027 due to a mathematical glitch in National Crime Records Bureau (NCRB) calculations. NCRB uses outdated Census population figures as denominators, causing crime rates to appear inflated until the next Census updates these numbers, leading to dramatic drops.
